title: Datadog INI Configuration Management

activities.name: cmd_config_get
steps: Call config_list($options) → group records by setting name (one setting may exist in multiple INI files)
[Branch] if not in records → echo '; <setting> = undefined; is missing in INI files'

activities.name: cmd_config_set
steps: For each CLI setting string in $options['d']:
parse_ini_setting($cliIniSetting) → [Branch] false → echo skip warning, continue
→ count=0: skip | count=false: echo error | count>0: echo success, $matchCount++
[Branch] $matchCount >= 1 → done (if >= 2: echo duplicate warning); continue next setting
→ count=0: skip | count=false: echo error | count>0: echo success, $matchCount++, break
[PASS 3 — create new entry in main INI file]
[Branch] file does not exist → attempt file_put_contents('') to create it; on failure echo error, continue
Read existing content; if not ending with EOL → append EOL
[Branch] write fails → echo error | success → echo success, $set=true

activities.name: config_list
steps: get_ini_settings('','','') → full array of DD INI setting definitions
For each INI file: parse_ini_file($path, false, INI_SCANNER_RAW) → raw settings
For each matched setting: create IniRecord(setting, currentValue, defaultValue, iniFile, binary)
Return $return array indexed by setting name (later entries overwrite earlier ones)

activities.name: update_ini_setting
steps: [Branch] $promoteComment=true → regex matches commented OR active lines (^[\h;]*key\h*=...)
[Branch] preg_replace returns null → return false (regex error)
[Branch] $count > 0 → file_put_contents($iniFile, newContent); if fails → return false

activities.name: find_all_ini_files
steps: Scan $phpProperties[INI_SCANDIR] for *.ini files; put 98-ddtrace.ini first in array
[Branch] INI_MAIN is set and is a file → use only that file as the list

activities.name: find_main_ini_files
steps: Scan directory for any .ini file containing 'extension = ddtrace' → use that filename instead
[Branch] Debian-based → also derive apache2/fpm SAPI paths
[Branch] INI_SCANDIR not set, INI_MAIN set → return [INI_MAIN]

activities.name: ini_values
steps: Parse lines split by '=>' for keys: PHP_VER, INI_MAIN, INI_SCANDIR, EXTENSION_DIR, THREAD_SAFETY, PHP_API, IS_DEBUG
Return associative array of PHP environment properties

activities.name: get_ini_settings
steps: Returns hardcoded array of all Datadog INI setting definitions
First 3 entries are 'extension' type (ddtrace, datadog-profiling, ddappsec) — skipped by config commands via CMD_CONFIG_NUM_SHIFT=3
Remaining entries are datadog.* settings with: name, default, commented, description

key_data_structures: IniRecord: {setting: string, currentValue: string, defaultValue: string, iniFile: string, binary: string, println()}
INI setting definition: {name: string, default: string, commented: bool, description: string|string[]}
parsed setting: [0: string key, 1: string value]
phpProperties: assoc array keyed by PHP_VER, INI_MAIN, INI_SCANDIR, EXTENSION_DIR, THREAD_SAFETY, PHP_API, IS_DEBUG
key_constants: CMD_CONFIG_NUM_SHIFT = 3 (extension= entries to skip in get_ini_settings output)
CMD_CONFIG_GET, CMD_CONFIG_SET, CMD_CONFIG_LIST (command discriminators in main())
OPT_PHP_BIN = '--php-bin' CLI option for selecting PHP binaries
branching_conditions: config set PASS 1: active match found in any INI file → update all, warn if duplicates
config set PASS 2: no active match → search commented lines, promote first occurrence only
config set PASS 3: no active or commented match → append to main INI file (create if missing)
config set: file creation fails → echo error, continue to next main file
config set: all main files fail → exit(1)
config get: setting name not in config_list results → print 'undefined; is missing in INI files'
Debian detection: INI_SCANDIR contains '/cli/conf.d' → also process apache2/fpm SAPI dirs
parse_ini_setting: not exactly 2 parts OR parse_ini_string fails → return false (skip setting)
call_graph: cmd_config_list → config_list → require_binaries_or_exit, get_ini_settings, ini_values, find_all_ini_files
cmd_config_set → require_binaries_or_exit, ini_values, parse_ini_setting, find_all_ini_files, update_ini_setting (×2 passes), find_main_ini_files
find_main_ini_files → (reads INI files to detect extension=ddtrace line)
important_notes: config list/get share the config_list() helper which indexes DD settings by name. A setting can appear in multiple INI files; get groups them while list overwrites (last wins). The 3-pass strategy in config set ensures existing active > commented > new-entry precedence. 98-ddtrace.ini is always prioritized (sorted first) in find_all_ini_files. Debian-based distros get SAPI-split INI dirs handled transparently.
source_files_analyzed: datadog-setup.php (functions: main, cmd_config_list, cmd_config_get, cmd_config_set, config_list, parse_ini_setting, update_ini_setting, find_all_ini_files, find_main_ini_files, ini_values, get_ini_settings, require_binaries_or_exit, filter_ssi_ini_paths, class IniRecord)
